channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.description:
1. Ice core reveals longest-ever continuous record of Earth’s climate  Nature
  2. What scientists found beneath Antarctica’s ice after drilling over 1,700 feet down  The Times of India
  3. Beyond EPICA's 1.2-million-year ice core links CO2 to Earth's ice age shift  동아사이언스
  4. Scientists extracted 228 meters of mud and rock under 523 meters of Antarctic ice — the largest sediment core ever recovered reveals 23 million years of the planet's climate history  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás

item.title: What it would have been like to experience the dinosaur-killing asteroid armageddon: a blow-by-blow account - The Conversation
item.description:
1. What it would have been like to experience the dinosaur-killing asteroid armageddon: a blow-by-blow account  The Conversation
  2. What we know about the colossal asteroid that wiped out dinosaurs  The Independent

item.title: JWST spots two early black holes growing far faster than their galaxies - Phys.org
item.description: JWST spots two early black holes growing far faster than their galaxies  Phys.orgSee more headlines & perspectives on Google News
